card_description: Robert Anton Wilson is a historical figure referenced in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. Appears in 2 Exegesis passages. Context: "Dick identifies a startling 1:1 correspondence between his 1967 story "Faith of Our Fathers" and his 3-74 experiences."

linked_segments.summary: Dick identifies a startling 1:1 correspondence between his 1967 story "Faith of Our Fathers" and his 3-74 experiences. He concludes that the Xerox letter was a "phony test/trap" (using "party jargon")
linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_74.txt

linked_segments.summary: Dick identifies the "horizontal form-axis" (orthogonal time) from his novel *Ubik* as an experientially real cosmological feature, proving Plato's theory of Forms. He refines his model of the "Urgrund
linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_136.txt
